title: pouteria samborae
intro: Pouteria samborae, also known as the Andina or 'Andina Tree', is a species of plant native to the tropical rainforests of South America, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its bark and seeds, which are rich in compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Pouteria samborae has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating gastrointestinal problems. The plant's bark and seeds are typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for their medicinal properties, making it a valuable resource for local communities in the Andean region.
